assertions.description: The analyzer correctly identifies two SY8009AAAC switching regulators (U2/U3) for the +3.3V and +1V2 rails respectively, detects the SPI configuration bus with prefix matching 'ICE40-', detects UART signals on PIO2_9/TxD and PIO2_8/RxD nets routed through U4 (the FPGA), identifies W25Q16 as a memory interface, and counts 5 power rails (+1V2, +3.3V, +5V, GND, PWR_FLAG). The 4-layer board stackup (F.Cu, In1(GND).Cu, In2(PWR).Cu, B.Cu) is also correctly parsed.

INCORRECT: The memory_interfaces detector reports connected_processors with shared_signal_nets=1 for the W25Q16 SPI flash connected to the iCE40HX1K FPGA. Inspection of net assignments shows that U1's DI, DO, CLK, CS, WP, and HOLD pins are all on unnamed/floating nets (e.g., __unnamed_16 through __unnamed_21), and only the CLK/SCK net ('iCE40-SCK') is actually shared with the FPGA. The SPI bus is effectively broken in the schematic — no MOSI/MISO/CS connections are made between U1 and U4. The analyzer's bus_analysis correctly identifies the SPI bus using only the iCE40-SD* prefix nets (which only have one end connected to U4/PGM1), but misses that U1 is not wired into this bus. This same issue appears in Rev A.

The schematic has a 10-pin programming connector (PGM1) whose pins are connected to the iCE40 SPI configuration bus nets (iCE40-SCK, iCE40-SDO, iCE40-SDI, iCE40-SS_B). This is a standard SPI programming header for iCE40 devices. The bus_analysis.spi section lists chip_select_count=1 but does not recognize PGM1 as a programming connector participant. The analyzer does not produce any JTAG or programming-connector detection. No jtag key exists in bus_analysis at all. The HX8K-EVB has an explicit JTAG connector (JTAG1, CON6) which is also not detected.
